rationale: Ran every empirical job at pinned HEAD 52d052d0. cmp of the two ms-panel.js copies exits 0 (byte-identical). go build ./... clean; go test ./plugins/mindspec -v: all 3 tests PASS (EmbedsMsPanel, AllowedCLIExactSet, new ShellMetacharRejectsBareDollar). git show --name-only HEAD lists exactly the 3 scoped files, one commit; git status stays clean throughout (scratch confined to /tmp). Extracted the live regex /[\x60;|&\n$]/ into a node harness: it matches (rejects) $HOME, ${x}, $x, backtick, ;, |, &, embedded newline, and $(x); it does NOT match p113, the full slug, bead/mindspec-x.1, or spec/113-x. Adversarial probe tried ${}, bare $, a$b, $$, escaped \$HOME, x=$y - all rejected, no bypass found. Traced every user-derived string (slug, spec, bead_id, target) through validateArgs and confirmed each routes through validateShellSafe before reaching buildCommand; mix.family is enum-restricted to claude/codex so no $ risk there, and slotId is internally generated, never user input. Mutation probe: copied the whole repo to /tmp, reverted the regex to the pre-fix /[\x60;|&\n]|\$(/, reran go test there - the new pin test genuinely reds (found 0 matches) while the other two floor tests stay green, proving the pin is not hollow.
findings: cmp plugins/mindspec/workflows/ms-panel.js .claude/workflows/ms-panel.js exits 0 - byte-identical mirror confirmed.

go build ./... and go test ./plugins/mindspec -v both green (3/3 tests pass): TestWorkflowFiles_EmbedsMsPanel, TestMsPanelWorkflow_AllowedCLIExactSet, TestMsPanelWorkflow_ShellMetacharRejectsBareDollar.
git show --name-only HEAD == exactly {.claude/workflows/ms-panel.js, plugins/mindspec/workflow_test.go, plugins/mindspec/workflows/ms-panel.js}; single commit; scope fence holds.

Node metachar matrix (extracted live regex /[\x60;|&\n$]/): MATCHES $HOME, ${x}, $x, a`b, a;b, a|b, a&b, embedded-newline, $(x); does NOT match p113, 113-panel-verb-workflow-followups, bead/mindspec-x.1, spec/113-x. Full matrix PASS.
Adversarial $-bypass hunt: ${}, $, a$b, $$, \$HOME, x=$y all rejected by the regex - no $-bearing value escapes the character class.

Bypass-to-buildCommand check: every value passed to buildCommand (slug, specId/spec, target, beadId) is validated via validateArgs -> validatePathElement + validateShellSafe (slug/spec), validateBeadId (folds in validateShellSafe), validateTarget (folds in validateShellSafe). mix[].family is enum-restricted to {"claude","codex"} so cannot carry a $. slotId (R1, R2, ...) is generated internally from a loop index, never from args. No path found where a $-bearing user value reaches buildCommand's template strings unvalidated.

Mutation probe (full repo copied to /tmp/r8-mutation-probe, mutated there only): reverting SHELL_METACHAR_RE to the pre-fix /[\x60;|&\n]|\$(/. in both the plugins and .claude copies makes TestMsPanelWorkflow_ShellMetacharRejectsBareDollar fail (found 0 occurrences of the fixed declaration) while the other two floor tests stay green - confirms the new pin is a real, non-hollow regression guard. A parallel node-level check on the reverted regex also confirmed $HOME/${x}/$x would bypass validateShellSafe under the old pattern, i.e. the original vulnerability the bead fixes was real.

git status clean throughout; no tracked file was ever modified (all mutation work done on a /tmp copy of the entire repo, since sandbox policy denies in-place edits to tracked files even for revert-and-restore probing).
